questions: Does the implementation correctly pass runId to the artifact summary endpoint?
Is the --run flag implementation correct and consistent with sibling commands?
Are there any regression risks from the changes?
Do all quality gates pass after the implementation?

answers.answer: Yes. The hxFetch call at bundle.ts:44-47 now includes queryParams: { runId }, passing the resolved runId to GET /api/tickets/:id/artifacts. The runId is guaranteed non-null after the process.exit guard at lines 38-41, making the unconditional pass both type-safe and correct.
evidence: bundle.ts:44-47: queryParams: { runId } added to hxFetch options
bundle.ts:37: runId resolved as explicitRunId ?? ticket.currentRun?.id ?? ticket.runs[0]?.id
bundle.ts:38-41: process.exit(1) guard ensures runId is string (not undefined)
http.ts:46-49: queryParams entries appended via url.searchParams.set(key, value)

answers.answer: Yes. The --run flag uses getFlag(args, '--run') at line 31, matching the pattern in artifact.ts:29. The precedence chain (explicitRunId ?? currentRun ?? runs[0]) is consistent with artifact.ts:35. The flag is documented in both the usage string (index.ts:27) and help text (index.ts:136).
evidence: bundle.ts:37: explicitRunId takes precedence in the nullish coalescing chain
artifact.ts:29: same getFlag(args, '--run') pattern used by sibling command

answers.answer: No regression risks identified. Passing runId unconditionally does not break active-status tickets because the server accepts runId for all statuses (verified by the working artifacts command). No shared utilities were modified. The getFlag import addition is additive. The flag parsing for --run does not interfere with existing --out parsing.
evidence: artifacts.ts:20-22: server already handles runId on the same endpoint for all statuses
bundle.ts:5: getFlag added alongside existing requireFlag import (additive change)
flags.ts:5-8: getFlag reads from args without mutation
bundle.ts:30-31: --out and --run parsed independently via separate getFlag/requireFlag calls

answers.answer: Yes. All three quality gates pass, independently verified by code review: npm run build (tsc, exit 0), npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit, exit 0), npm test (30/30 tests pass, exit 0).
evidence: Code review ran npm run build: exit 0
Code review ran npm run typecheck: exit 0
Code review ran npm test: 30/30 tests pass, 0 failures, exit 0

statement: Code review complete. All changed files (bundle.ts, index.ts) reviewed against ticket requirements, product spec, and sibling command patterns. Implementation is correct: runId is passed to the artifact summary endpoint via queryParams, --run flag is added with proper precedence, and usage/help documentation is updated. No issues found, no code changes needed. All quality gates pass independently.
